ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_03301638 represents cola soft drink as a category; no Pepsi-brand-specific URI exists in FoodOn. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 AN000I covers cola-type soft drinks as a category. No Pepsi-specific branded code exists in FoodEx2; closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226465004 represents cola drink as a substance category; no Pepsi-brand-specific code exists. Closest parent used.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard 330 ml can of Pepsi Cola, equivalent to approximately 341 g, as commonly served in Singapore and convenience outlets.

nutrient_source.title: Pepsi-Cola, carbonated beverage — USDA FoodData Central, FDC ID 174852
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA FoodData Central FDC ID 174852 is the branded Pepsi-Cola carbonated beverage entry. Values are per 100 g (approximately 100 ml) of the beverage. Serving size scaled to 330 ml (341 g) for GL calculation.

gi_source.title: Soft drink, Pepsi Cola — Glycemic Index Database, University of Sydney
gi_source.source_match_notes: The University of Sydney GI Database reports a GI of 63 for Pepsi Cola (glucose reference = 100). GL per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (63 × 36.1) / 100 ≈ 22.8. Carbohydrate per 330 ml serving = 10.6 g per 100 g × 3.41 = 36.1 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content (11 mg per 100 g; approximately 37 mg per 330 ml can)
hypertension.guidance: Pepsi Cola is low in sodium and does not directly raise blood pressure through sodium load; however, its high sugar content may contribute to weight gain and metabolic risk, which are secondary drivers of hypertension, so regular consumption should be limited.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High free sugar content (approximately 36 g per 330 ml can) and medium glycemic index (GI 63)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid or strictly limit Pepsi Cola due to its high free sugar load and medium GI of 63, which produces a glycemic load of approximately 22.8 per can and can cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High free sugar intake linked to elevated triglycerides, visceral adiposity, and cardiovascular risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Regular consumption of sugar-sweetened beverages such as Pepsi Cola is associated with elevated serum triglycerides and increased cardiovascular disease risk; individuals with CVD should limit intake to occasional consumption and prefer water or unsweetened beverages.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Pepsi Cola (330 ml can) is a globally recognised carbonated cola soft drink manufactured by PepsiCo. It is an ultra-processed, ready-to-drink beverage produced by combining carbonated water with high-fructose corn syrup or sucrose, caramel colour, phosphoric acid, natural cola flavours, and caffeine. The drink is characterised by a sweet, slightly acidic taste with a distinct cola flavour profile and effervescent mouthfeel. It contains no protein, no fat, and no dietary fibre; its caloric content (approximately 41 kcal per 100 g, or 136 kcal per 330 ml can) derives entirely from free sugars (approximately 36 g per can). The glycemic index is 63 (medium) per the University of Sydney GI Database, yielding a glycemic load of approximately 22.8 per standard can — clinically significant for blood glucose management. Pepsi Cola is widely available in Singapore, hawker centres, and convenience stores, including as a menu item at Italian and Western dining establishments. From a clinical nutrition perspective, it is a high-concern beverage for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to its free sugar load, and a moderate concern for cardiovascular disease due to its association with elevated triglycerides and metabolic syndrome when consumed regularly. It provides negligible micronutrient value. The product is suitable for vegetarians and vegans and is gluten-free and dairy-free.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a can of Pepsi?
How much sugar is in a 330ml Pepsi?
What is the GI of Pepsi Cola?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from USDA FoodData Central FDC ID 174852, which is an exact branded Pepsi-Cola entry. GI value of 63 sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database for Pepsi Cola specifically. Both are approved primary sources under Project Veritas v2 source policy.
notes: Protein_g is confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central FDC ID 174852; contains no protein as Pepsi Cola is a sugar-water carbonated beverage with no protein-containing ingredients. Fat_g is confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central; contains no fat by product formulation. Dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero as carbonated soft drinks contain no dietary fibre. Cholesterol_mg is confirmed zero. The cuisine field in the input record was listed as 'Italian' reflecting the restaurant context (Pasta Fresca Da Salvatore); however, Pepsi Cola is a globally manufactured US-origin product and has been reclassified to 'Global / International' accordingly. Multilingual names provided for Chinese (Simplified and Traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi as the product is widely sold in Singapore and the input source is field survey data. Monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, and trans_fat_g are 0.0 confirmed zero per USDA source as the beverage contains no fat fraction. Vitamin values reported as 0.0 are confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central for this product. GL per serving = (63 × 36.1) / 100 = 22.8, where 36.1 g carbohydrate is derived from 10.6 g/100g × 341 g serving weight.

improvement_actions_taken: Corrected cuisine from 'Italian' (restaurant context artifact) to 'Global / International' (product origin).
Added zero-macro justification phrases in notes for protein_g and fat_g per zero-macro justification rule.
Set glycemic_index=63 (medium) from University of Sydney GI Database exact match for Pepsi Cola.
Calculated glycemic_load_per_serving=22.8 based on 330 ml serving (341 g) and 10.6 g/100g carbohydrate.
Provided multilingual local_names and aliases for Chinese (Simplified and Traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi reflecting Singapore distribution context.
Verified aliases_by_language and flat aliases list are perfectly consistent.
Used only allowed ontology match_type values: exact, closest_parent, none.
Documented all sentinel -1 replacements: all micronutrients set to 0.0 where USDA reports confirmed zero, or to actual numeric values where reported.
